title: Trying to understand SoFi — feels like the market is split here
author: topicspace
selftext:
I’ve been tracking how narratives shift across AI/tech over the past couple months (ended up looking at \~13k events across news, Reddit, etc.).

One thing I’ve noticed is that the most interesting setups aren’t the clean ones — it’s when the market clearly disagrees.

SoFi feels like one of those.

On the surface, the bull case is straightforward:

* revenue looks like it’s re-accelerating
* stock is down \~50% from highs
* CEO (Noto) bought \~$1M of stock recently

But at the same time, Muddy Waters just put out a short report.

So now you’ve got:

* insider buying
* vs a credible short thesis

Both showing up at the same time.

That’s what makes it confusing — both sides look high conviction.

Feels less like a simple “buy the dip” and more like a disagreement trade.

Curious how others are thinking about it —  
is this where mispricing tends to show up, or is one side clearly missing something?
